extraction.one_line_summary: DÉMARE Arnaud won after KEISSE Iljo executed Tour de France-style leadout for Deceuninck-Quick-Step with 1km positioning advantage over rivals
tactical_patterns: KEISSE Iljo replicated Tour de France leadout positioning for DÉMARE with 1km organization while rivals messy.
ULISSI Diego aggressively defended first intermediate sprint against SAGAN Peter, yielding only one point at 70km.
GAVIRIA Fernando, VIVIANI Elia, HODEG Álvaro José all dropped pre-summit 6-7km before climb top, exposing positioning weakness.

rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: positive: Won from superior team organization rather than pure speed, benefiting from KEISSE's Tour-proven positioning
rider_signals.KEISSE Iljo: positive: Executed high-value leadout work at 1km mark that neutralized SAGAN's early launch
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Launched first in final kilometer but poor positioning meant effort wasted against organized train
rider_signals.ULISSI Diego: positive: Actively defended Maglia Ciclamino at intermediate sprint 70km out, showing willingness to contest small margins
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: Dropped 6-7km before summit on moderate climb, revealing insufficient positioning or climbing for mixed stages
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: Dropped on climb but regained contact on descent, indicating tactical positioning failure rather than form issue
